message.content: Find the most recent performance.csv file under /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv/. List the top 30 functions sorted by cycle count (highest first). The CSV format is: group, function, inst, cycle. Skip lines where cycle is "x". Return the full list with group, function, inst, cycle columns.

input.description: Extract top 30 functions by cycle count, skip "x" entries

content.content:
awk: cmd. line:1: NR>1 && $4 \!= "x" && $4 \!= "" {print $0}
awk: cmd. line:1:            ^ backslash not last character on line

awk: cmd. line:1: NR>1 && $4 \!= "x" && $4 \!= "" {print $0}
awk: cmd. line:1:            ^ backslash not last character on line

input.command:
python3 -c "
import csv
rows = []
with open('/home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv/test_sh_20260320150527/performance.csv') as f:
    reader = csv.reader(f)
    header = next(reader)
    for r in reader:
        if len(r) >= 4 and r[3].strip() != 'x' and r[3].strip() != '':
            try:
                cycle = int(r[3].strip())
                rows.append((r[0].strip(), r[1].strip(), r[2].strip(), cycle))
            except ValueError:
                pass
rows.sort(key=lambda x: x[3], reverse=True)
print(f'{'group':<30} {'function':<65} {'inst':>12} {'cycle':>12}')
print('-'*121)
for g, fn, inst, cy in rows[:30]:
    print(f'{g:<30} {fn:<65} {inst:>12} {cy:>12}')
"
input.description: Parse CSV with Python and display top 30 by cycle count
